title: Target state: invoke pi-context-cli as the first-class `pi-context` command, not `node .../dist/bin.js`
description: packages/pi-context-cli/package.json already declares `bin: { "pi-context": "./dist/bin.js" }` and dist/bin.js carries a `#!/usr/bin/env node` shebang — so installing or linking the package puts a first-class `pi-context` command on PATH. But the dev workflow, the docs (CLAUDE.md, READMEs), the dogfooding invocations, and orchestration all call `node packages/pi-context-cli/dist/bin.js <op>` — the developer-mode path-to-bin wart, not a real CLI. TARGET STATE: pi-context is a first-rate command invoked as `pi-context <op> --...` everywhere; no `node /path/to/bin` ceremony.

evidence.reference: bin: { pi-context: ./dist/bin.js } — the first-class command is already declared
#!/usr/bin/env node shebang present — directly executable once on PATH
the canonical filing patterns + dogfooding all show `node packages/pi-context-cli/dist/bin.js <op>` rather than `pi-context <op>`

impact: Every CLI invocation (docs, dogfooding, scripts, other-agent shell access) carries the path-to-bin wart; the CLI does not present as a first-rate installed command, which is friction, non-portable, and a poorer dogfooding/UX signal than a real `pi-context <op>` command.
proposed_resolution: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
